etymology.description: semantic 亠(亠) (symbol for high position) + phonetic 工(工, sound) -> high
etymology.interpretation: The character 亢(kàng, "high") combines the semantic element 亠(亠) representing a high position or elevated point with the phonetic component 工(工), which contributes the 'kàng' sound. This combination suggests something elevated or high.

examples.chinese: 在高亢的山顶上(在高亢的山頂上)，我们可以俯瞰整个城市(我們可以俯瞰整個城市)。
examples.pinyin: Zài gāokàng de shāndǐng shàng, wǒmen kěyǐ fǔkàn zhěnggè chéngshì.
examples.english: At the high mountain peak, we can overlook the entire city.

examples.chinese: 他的声调很亢(他的聲調很亢)，在唱歌时非常有力(在唱歌時非常有力)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de shēngdiào hěn kàng, zài chànggē shí fēicháng yǒulì.
examples.english: His tone is high and very powerful when singing.
